data.title: Japan eyes new tax to fund infrastructure fixes to replace gasoline surcharge, Asahi says
data.description:
Japan's government is considering introducing a new tax to secure funding for the repair of aging public infrastructure to replace a decades-old gasoline surcharge to be abolished by the end of the year, the Asahi newspaper reported on Monday.

The potential new levy is likely to target car drivers, replacing the so-called "provisional" gasoline tax which Japan's ruling coalition agreed with the opposition to abolish "at the soonest this year."

The provisional tax, introduced in the 1970s originally as a temporary step to fund road building and maintenance, adds 25.1 yen ($0.17) per litre to the base rate of 28.7 yen. The end of the tax would mean a loss of about 1 trillion yen annually in general tax revenue.

The finance ministry and internal affairs ministry will be drafting initial plans, which may include new fuel-based taxation, the Asahi reported, without citing sources. Revenues would be distributed to local governments, which bear the bulk of infrastructure maintenance costs, it said.

But the new tax could draw criticism from opposition lawmakers as merely a rebranding of the expiring gasoline levy that undermines efforts to ease the burden on consumers amid rising living costs, the Asahi said.

The Ministry of Finance did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the report.

($1 = 147.3800 yen)

data.title: Support for Japan PM Ishiba surges despite election defeat, poll shows
data.description:
Support for Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba surged nearly 20 percentage points, a Yomiuri newspaper public opinion poll showed on Monday, despite his ruling coalition losing its majority in July's parliamentary election.

Support for Ishiba's cabinet stood at 39%, surging by 17 percentage points from last month's survey taken after the upper house election. The disapproval rate fell to 50% from July's 67%.

The public approval for the prime minister likely bounced thanks to factors such as Japan reaching a trade deal with the United States and the government's announcement of increasing rice production to counter sharp price rise, the newspaper reported.

Ishiba vowed to stay in his post after the election defeat, fending off demands by some of his Liberal Democratic Party lawmakers to resign to take responsibility.

Asked whether Ishiba should step down following the election results, 42% said "yes," down from last month's 54%, the Yomiuri survey showed.

Fifty percent of respondents said Ishiba did not need to resign, up from 35% in the previous poll.

data.title: NZX Price Target Cut 1.3% to NZ$1.50/Share by Forsyth Barr
ASX 200 stocks hitting fresh 52-week highs and lows – Week 35
data.description:
Welcome back to the 52-Week Series, where we recap the S&P/ASX 200 stocks that have hit fresh yearly highs and lows over the past week. Tracking this data point helps identify emerging trends across various stocks and sectors.

When clusters of stocks within a sector reach new highs or lows, it often points to powerful underlying drivers — such as shifting commodity prices, changing demand patterns (such as AI, pivots into defensive sectors, consumer weakness etc), or companies beating/missing earnings expectations.

52-Week Highs and Lows by Sector

  • Real Estate: 12 High, 0 Low

  • Industrials: 9 High, 0 Low

  • Financials: 8 High, 1 Low

  • Discretionary: 7 High, 1 Low

  • Materials: 6 High, 2 Low

  • Telecommunications: 3 High, 0 Low

  • Technology: 2 High, 0 Low

  • Utilities: 2 High, 1 Low

  • Health Care: 1 High, 1 Low

  • Energy: 1 High, 1 Low

  • Staples: 1 High, 1 Low

What Does the Data Tell Us?

  • Massive breadth uplift saw 52 (26%) of S&P/ASX 200 stocks rally to fresh yearly highs. The market crossed 9,000 for the first time last week, with year-to-date returns hitting double digits for the first time this year. While defensive sectors like Staples, Healthcare and Utilities continue to lag, growing rate cut expectations are driving outsized gains in rate-sensitive Real Estate sector, as well as Discretionary and Financials.

  • Industrial stocks delivered strong numbers, with results from Brambles, Monadelphous, Downer and Transurban all well received by the market.

  • Big Four divergence saw strong gains from ANZ, Westpac and NAB, while CBA struggles—now down around 10% from its 25-June all-time high.

data.title: Zip's Bull Stays Positive on Margin Outlook — Market Talk
data.description: Zip's bull at UBS remains positive on the buy-now-pay-later provider's margin outlook. Analyst Tim Piper keeps a buy rating on the stock, telling clients in a note that strong U.S. growth and low loss rates suggest that its fiscal 2026 margin guidance is relatively conservative. The Australian company's operating leverage is strong in current conditions, he observes. Piper adds that the stock is trading at about 32X earnings on an adjusted basis, which he sees as attractive given his expectation of 27% compound annual earnings growth over the next three years. UBS lifts its target price 32% to A$4.50. Shares are up 6.7% at A$4.00. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Perenti Bull Cheers Strong Cash Generation, Earnings Beat — Market Talk
data.description: Perenti delivers solid free cash generation and an earnings beat, Citi analyst William Park says in a note following the company's FY result. Shares are up 7.7% at A$2.25. There are no real surprises for FY 2025 given Perenti's recent guidance on free cash for the year, says Park. He says the "earnings beat and optimistic outlook, particularly regarding free cash guidance in FY26, should dominate headlines" on the stock. Citi has a buy rating and A$2.20 stock target. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Australia's Southern Cross Media soars on upbeat FY results
data.description:
** Southern Cross Media soars as much as 22.7% to A$0.810; set for best session since June 20, 2023

** Stock hits highest since May 28, 2024

** Media firm reports FY revenue of ~A$422 mln ($273.4 mln), up 5% y/y

** Posts FY underlying net profit attributable of A$15.1 mln, above year-ago A$4.5 mln

** YTD, SXL gains more than 30%

($1 = A$1.5430)

data.title: Zip Target Price Raised 32% to A$4.50/Share by UBS>ZIP.AU
Brent Holds Advance
data.description:
Brent crude oil futures hovered near $68 per barrel on Monday, holding gains from last week as investors continued to weigh supply risks and prospects of US monetary easing.

The US threatened to double tariffs on all imports from India to 50% in retaliation for New Delhi’s continued purchases of Russian crude, fueling concerns over potential supply disruptions.

However, markets are also considering possible demand implications from India, a key consumer, after officials signaled refiners would keep sourcing oil from Moscow despite the penalties due Wednesday.

Adding to uncertainty, stalled Russia-Ukraine peace talks heightened fears of supply disruptions as both countries stepped up attacks on each other's energy infrastructure.

Meanwhile, Fed Chair Jerome Powell suggested Friday that rate cuts could resume as soon as next month, boosting risk sentiment and supporting expectations for stronger crude demand amid improved economic activity and a weaker US dollar.

data.title: SATS' Near-Term Earnings Visibility Likely Strengthened — Market Talk
data.description: SATS' near-term earnings visibility likely strengthened, DBS Group Research's Jason Sum says in a research report. Its drivers are cargo strength, margin improvement in its Food Solutions segment, and resilient ground handling and aviation catering volumes, the analyst says. Also, the partnership between the provider of gateway services and food solutions and Japan's Mitsui, together with an expanding product portfolio, should support its growth in non-aviation food. DBS lifts its FY 2026 and FY 2027 core net profit forecasts for SATS by 10% and 7%, respectively. It raises the stock's target price to S$3.80 from S$3.50 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 0.3% higher at S$3.27. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Rakon Price Target Raised 28% to NZ$0.91/Share by Forsyth Barr
Kossan Rubber Industries Could Post Stronger 2H on U.S. Restocking — Market Talk
data.description: Kossan Rubber Industries is expected to post stronger 2H earnings, supported by firmer selling prices, cost savings and a demand recovery, CIMB Securities analyst Walter Aw Lik Hsin says in a note. U.S. clients restocking after running down inventories built ahead of tariffs on Chinese gloves could help support earnings, he says. With more than half its 1H revenue coming from North America, Kossan is well-positioned to benefit from this restocking cycle, though the recovery may be gradual amid tougher competition in other markets and rising Chinese capacity. CIMB cuts Kossan's target price to MYR1.65 from MYR2.10, citing a reversion to historical valuation multiples amid a still-muted industry outlook and rising competition. It keeps a buy rating on the stock, which is 0.8% higher at MYR1.24. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Tencent's New Game Showing Strong Momentum Since Launch — Market Talk
data.description: Tencent's "Valorant Mobile" has shown strong momentum since its launch, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a research note. The game is currently ranked No. 1 among overall iOS apps, No. 1 in game downloads and No. 7 in game grossing in China, the analysts note. It is on track to meet MS's expectation of top five grossing, with around 5 billion yuan-6 billion yuan of grossing in the first year, they say. The MS analysts think Tencent could further capture market share in the first-person shooter genre in China after the successful recent launch of "Delta Force" and now "Valorant Mobile." MS is "incrementally more confident in Tencent games after 2Q" given the sustainable performance of its evergreen games and strong deferred revenue of international games. Tencent shares last closed at HK$600.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)
